what_it_does: This file is a thin Node.js ESM executable shim that spawns a sibling script, mcp-server-wrapper.js, with the caller's arguments and inherited stdio, then exits with the child's exit code.
observations: Uses a shebang (#!/usr/bin/env node) so it can be run as a CLI binary.
Polyfills CommonJS __filename and __dirname using import.meta.url because ESM does not provide them.
Spawns process.execPath (the current Node binary) to run mcp-server-wrapper.js.
Passes process.argv.slice(2) so the wrapper receives the same CLI arguments.
Uses stdio: 'inherit' so the wrapper shares the parent's terminal streams.
Forwards the child exit code through process.exit(code ?? 0).
standouts: It is a pure passthrough launcher with no business logic of its own.
It deliberately re-executes a sibling wrapper rather than running the server directly.
conventions: Follows the CLI shim pattern of shebang + spawn wrapper + forward exit code.
Uses stdio inheritance for transparent terminal behavior.
concerns: No error handling if mcp-server-wrapper.js is missing or spawn fails.
No explicit forwarding of signals (SIGINT/SIGTERM) from parent to child.
process.exit may terminate the parent before async cleanup completes.
notes: A minimal ESM CLI shim that delegates to mcp-server-wrapper.js and mirrors its arguments, stdio, and exit status.
questions: What functionality does mcp-server-wrapper.js add that requires this separate shim?
Is the wrapper expected to be installed next to this file at runtime?
Are signals intended to be forwarded, or is stdio inheritance considered sufficient?
purpose_vs_actual: No stated purpose is present in the file, but its behavior is consistent with a CLI launcher/wrapper.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to provide a stable, executable CLI entry point that resolves to a sibling wrapper script while preserving the exact command-line invocation environment.
contribution: Provides an executable, ESM-compatible launcher that forwards CLI invocation to the wrapper script, keeping the server implementation separate and preserving argument/stdio/exit behavior.
summary_for_embedding: company/recollection/cli/mcp-server is a Node.js ESM CLI shim that spawns mcp-server-wrapper.js with inherited stdio and forwards the child process exit code.
intention_for_embedding: To expose a command-line entry point that delegates execution to a sibling wrapper script while preserving arguments and terminal interaction.
